cit.basis: Estonian model since 1 January 2018: no annual CIT on retained or reinvested profit; 20% CIT on the grossed-up base only when profit is distributed (dividends and deemed distributions), effectively 25% of the net distribution.
cit.note: Deemed distributions (non-business expenses, transfer-pricing adjustments, excessive interest, loans to related parties) are taxed like dividends — simplified out of this SME estimator.
cit.pillar_two_note: Latvia applies the EU Pillar Two (Directive 2022/2523) 15% global minimum tax for groups with consolidated revenue >= EUR 750m (deferred IIR/UTPR election) — out of scope for this SME estimator.

vat.note: EU member state; One-Stop-Shop (OSS/IOSS) available for cross-border B2C supplies.

official_sources.name: State Revenue Service (VID, Valsts ienemumu dienests)

effective_date_range.basis: Target ruleset/params year; jurisdiction fiscal calendars and transitional periods may differ.

rate_threshold_assumptions: Rates and thresholds are simplified for LedgerField estimates.
Deemed-distribution items, credit-institution surcharge, participation exemption, and sector-specific rules may alter outcomes.
Verify against official guidance and local advisor before filing or production use.
limitations: Not legal, tax, accounting, or filing advice.
Does not encode every deduction, exemption, treaty, WHT, payroll, or filing rule.
High-stakes use requires local validation against official forms and current law.
